channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AFCON 2027: How East Africa Is Preparing For a Big Party While Nervous About Guest Lists
item.description: The article talks about the pressure and worry that Tanzania, Uganda and Kenya feel as they prepare to co‑host the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON). The writers explain that the three countries are ...

item.title: Greg Rickford's Role in Advancing Northern Ontario Development and Indigenous Partnerships
item.description: Greg Rickford, Ontario’s Minister of Indigenous Affairs, is leading efforts to accelerate economic and community development in northern Ontario, particularly the Ring of Fire region. With a backgroun...

item.title: Tensions Rise in Superannuation Sector Over Young Members Switching Funds
item.description: Tensions are rising in Australia’s superannuation sector, with two of the country’s largest retail super funds dismissing new research claiming young Australians are increasingly moving from tradition...

item.title: Meningitis Cases in Kent Drop to 29 After Reclassification of Earlier Reports
item.description: The meningitis outbreak in Kent has shown its first sign of decline, with total confirmed and suspected cases dropping from 34 to 29, including two deaths — a 21-year-old University of Kent student an...

item.title: Police officer Bradley Corke, 27, dies responding to emergency call in Kent crash
item.description: PC Bradley Corke, a 27-year-old officer with Kent Police, has died following a serious road collision in Farningham while responding to an emergency call. The incident occurred on Thursday evening aro...

item.title: Gauteng Opens New Food Distribution Centre to Tackle Poverty and Create Jobs
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Social Development has unveiled a new state-run food distribution centre in Centurion, aiming to combat hunger and poverty in the region. This facility, the first of its kind...

item.title: Police Arrest Four Suspects Linked to Burglary Operations in Akwa Ibom
item.description: Police for Akwa Ibom don arrest four people involved in burglary activities for the state. On March 17, 2026, two suspects, Idiongho Johnny (29) and Ofonime Jimmy (30), were arrested for shop burglary...

item.title: Iran issues warning over attacks on energy infrastructure following Trump’s Strait of Hormuz threat
item.description: The situation between the US and Iran has escalated as Iran warned that its energy and oil infrastructure would be considered 'legitimate targets' and destroyed should further attacks occur. This came...

item.title: Saturday Night Live UK Receives Mixed but Positive Reviews After Promising Debut
item.description: The UK version of Saturday Night Live premiered on Sky One to largely positive but cautious reviews from critics. Hosted by Tina Fey and featuring performances from 11 emerging comedians and the band ...

item.title: Pakistan Super League 11 to be held in Lahore and Karachi without spectators amid austerity measures
item.description: The Pakistan Super League (PSL) 11th edition is all set to start from 26th March, but this time, fans won’t be allowed in the stadiums due to the government’s austerity drive. PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqv...

item.title: Trump warns of military action against Iran if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening attacks on the country's power plants if Tehran does not allow the free passage of oil tankers through the Strait of ...

item.title: Politician’s Bank Accounts Frozen Amid Political Fallout Ahead of 2027 Polls
item.description: As Kenya gears up for the 2027 elections, political battles are heating up, with reports showing that two accounts belonging to a prominent politician have been frozen—allegedly because of his recent ...

item.title: KZN ANC Youth League Leadership Facing Scrutiny Amidst Internal Dispute
item.description: The KwaZulu-Natal (KZN) ANC Youth League is currently embroiled in a significant internal power struggle, prompting national leadership intervention. Following a recent tumultuous meeting, the provinc...

item.title: Roman villa unearthed in Wales offers rare insight into ancient life
item.description: Archaeologists in Wales have uncovered the ruins of a Roman villa that experts are comparing to a mini-Pompeii due to its remarkable state of preservation. The site, believed to date back to the Roman...

item.title: Critique of Paul Ehrlich's Population Bomb and Its Global Impact
item.description: Father Raymond J. de Souza's opinion piece sharply criticizes Paul Ehrlich, author of the 1968 book The Population Bomb, who died on March 13, 2026, at age 93. Ehrlich predicted massive famines in the...

item.title: Kucherov overtakes McDavid to lead NHL scoring race after four‑point game in Lightning win over Oilers
item.description: Nikita Kucherov put together another dominant performance to move into the NHL scoring lead, collecting two goals and two assists as the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Edmonton Oilers 5‑2 in Edmonton. W...

item.title: Tensions Escalate: Trump Sets Ultimatum to Iran Amidst West Asia Conflict
item.description: The situation in West Asia is escalating rapidly, with US President Donald Trump issuing a stark ultimatum to Iran. Trump has given Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, threatening to “oblite...

item.title: PCB Announces PSL 11 Without Spectators, Restricted to Lahore and Karachi Due to Austerity Measures
item.description: Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) Chairman Mohsin Naqvi announced that the 11th edition of the Pakistan Super League (PSL) will proceed as scheduled starting March 26, but without spectators and limited to...

item.title: Liz Jones Reflects on Personal Financial Pressures Despite Career Success
item.description: In her column for You Magazine, Liz Jones shares a candid account of her current financial struggles, despite a successful career in journalism. Writing on 2 March, she reveals she had only £40 left i...

item.title: Fuel supply remains stable amid calls to avoid panic buying
item.description: The Fuels Industry Association of South Africa (FIASA) has assured motorists that the country’s fuel supply remains stable, with no national shortages reported. Spokesperson Phila Mzamo confirmed that...
